items.title: Robert K. Irving, prominent Canadian businessman who led J.D. Irving Ltd. and Cavendish Farms, dead at 71
items.summary_auto: A leading member of New Brunswick's Irving family has died. Robert K. Irving, who was co-CEO of J.D. Irving Ltd., died Tuesday.

items.title: PWHL adds expansion franchise in San Jose, bringing league to 12 teams
items.summary_auto: The PWHL will add a franchise in San Jose ahead of next season, bringing the league to 12 teams. San Jose joins Detroit, Hamilton and Las Vegas as the four expansion teams heading into the league's fourth season.

items.title: Report on Churchill Falls MOU says deal not in N.L.’s best interest: Source
Canada's annual inflation rate rose to 2.8% in April, thanks to soaring energy prices
items.summary_auto: Cost of gasoline was 28.6 per cent higher in April year-over-year, helping boost overall inflation, while the pace of inflation slowed for other costs like rent and food. Economists noted that core measures of inflation that strip out more volatile prices have risen at a much slower clip.

items.title: Todd Blanche expected to be grilled over Trump administration's $1.8B fund blasted as 'corruption on steroids'
items.summary_auto: Todd Blanche, acting attorney general in Donald Trump's administration, will appear on Capitol Hill on Tuesday, and is expected to face intense scrutiny over Justice Department plans to create a $1.776-billion US fund to pay allies of the Republican president who believe they were targeted politically.

items.title: Closing arguments underway in 1st-degree murder trial of Dean Penney
WHO concerned about 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak, with 131 now dead
items.summary_auto: At least 131 deaths and over 500 suspected cases have been reported in the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo, the Congolese health ministry said Tuesday as the World Health Organization's head expressed concern over the "scale and speed of the epidemic."

items.title: How should Canada handle Alberta separatism?
items.summary_auto: A conversation about separatist movements, and what makes them legitimate in the eyes of the law, public and history.

items.title: 'It's appalling': Halifax women condemn 15-month wait for mammograms
items.summary_auto: Cynthia McCutcheon thought she heard wrong when she was told the next available appointment for a mammogram in the Halifax area was in July, 2027. She's worried some cancers will be diagnosed at later stages because of the backlog in Nova Scotia.

items.title: My service dog has given me my life back. Distracting him can take that away
items.summary_auto: Rachel Lyons’s service dog, Geneva, helps her navigate the world, and often garners attention from strangers. But when people try to distract Geneva, it can cause medical issues for her.

items.title: Montreal F1 stripper strike part of fight to legitimize and decriminalize sex work
items.summary_auto: Montreal strippers say that for too long, their employment as independent contractors has shielded strip club owners from ensuring safe working conditions. They're striking on Saturday in a show of collective action unprecedented in recent Canadian history.

items.title: Ontario touts $8.5B annual economic impact from Toronto Island airport expansion — but won't show the math
items.summary_auto: The Ontario government says expending Billy Bishop airport could provide an $8.5-billion annual economic boost by 2050. But weeks after first touting that claim, neither the province nor the Toronto Port Authority, which owns the airport, has provided any evidence to support it. Several experts say they're skeptical.

items.title: Trump is gunning for Republican Thomas Massie, who defied him on Epstein files
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is yet again aiming to show he has an iron grip on the Republican Party, pushing to oust the Republican congressman who stood up to him on the Epstein files.

items.title: At 'crucial' moment for pandemic preparedness, experts say world isn't ready
items.summary_auto: Government funding cutbacks and a distrust in public health authorities means the world is not ready for the next pandemic, according to a report released Monday by the Global Preparedness Monitoring Board (GPMB).

items.title: Canadiens advance to Eastern Conference final with OT win over Sabres in Game 7
items.summary_auto: The Montreal Canadiens are heading to the Eastern Conference final after defeating the Buffalo Sabres 3-2 in overtime in Game 7 on Monday night at KeyBank Center.

items.title: Mark Fuhrman, L.A. police detective in O.J. Simpson murder case, dead at 74
items.summary_auto: Former Los Angeles police detective Mark Fuhrman, who was convicted of lying during testimony at the O.J. Simpson murder trial, has died. Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of Simpson's ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.

items.title: N.W.T. battles 3 wildfires as season gets underway
items.summary_auto: The government says it is fighting three fires in the Northwest Territories, two of which are described as human caused. It says a human-caused wildfire in the South Slave region is under control.

items.title: Edmonton councillor wants to rename road near Alberta's legislature after pro-federalist movement
items.summary_auto: An Edmonton city councillor says he wants to ingrain the love Albertans have for Canada in a red-and-white street sign.

items.title: U.S. slaps tariffs on fresh Canadian mushrooms, claims growers getting unfair subsidies
items.summary_auto: The United States has put countervailing duties on fresh mushrooms grown in Canada following a U.S. Department of Commerce investigation which the Canadian industry has called "deeply flawed."

items.title: Irish councillor proposes 'smallest statue' for mosquito that killed Oliver Cromwell
items.summary_auto: A city councillor in Cork, Ireland is advocating to erect a public statue of a mosquito. It wouldn’t be a statue of just any mosquito , but the pest that some — at least in Cork–believe bit the English invader, Oliver Cromwell — sending him home to die of what was once known as 'Cork fever,' an old name for malaria.

items.title: 'Smallest statue in the world': Irish councillor proposes monument for mosquito that killed Cromwell
items.summary_auto: A city councillor in Cork, Ireland is advocating to erect a public statue of a mosquito. It wouldn’t be a statue of just any mosquito , but the pest that some — at least in Cork–believe bit the English invader, Oliver Cromwell — sending him home to die of what was once known as 'Cork fever,' an old name for malaria.

items.title: 3 killed in California mosque shooting, 2 suspects also dead: authorities
World Cup heats up gentrification battle in Mexico City
items.summary_auto: The looming FIFA World Cup is intensifying gentrification in Mexico City, where advocates say landlords are pushing tenants out to convert long-term homes into short-term rentals — hoping to make a quick buck from tourist demand.

items.title: Trump says he called off Tuesday Iran strike at request of Gulf allies
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump said he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because "serious negotiations" are underway. Trump's announcement in a social media post Monday came as he had threatened the clock was ticking for Iran to strike a deal or fighting would renew after a fragile ceasefire.

items.title: Greenland's self-determination is non-negotiable, PM tells Trump envoy
items.summary_auto: Greenland's Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen said Monday that he had a respectful and positive meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump 's special envoy to the Arctic territory, but that he made it clear that the Greenlandic people continue to insist on self-determination.

items.title: Manitoba selling off shelved U.S. booze that's set to expire in fall
items.summary_auto: There weren't any lineups but there were mile-long smiles from Winnipeggers who took advantage of another sale of U.S. booze at Manitoba Liquor Marts on Monday — the first time the province has offered it up since December.
